title: pholidocarpus ihur
intro: Pholidocarpus ihur, also known as Kukui nut tree, is a plant species native to the Pacific Islands and Southeast Asia, recognized for its unique characteristics and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its seeds, which are used to produce a fixed oil that exhibits medicinal properties. The oil extracted from Pholidocarpus ihur seeds has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, providing relief from skin conditions, wounds, and other health issues. In herbal preparations, the oil is often used topically or added to traditional remedies to harness its benefits, which include soothing skin irritations, promoting wound healing, and reducing pain and inflammation.
